title: elymus rhodopaeus
intro: Elymus rhodopaeus, also known as Rhodope wildrye or Rhodope wheat grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Bulgaria, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. Elymus rhodopaeus is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or dried powder for medicinal purposes, making it a versatile addition to traditional folk remedies.
